_warning: HAND-AUTHORED. A sync script that writes this file is a bug. No upstream source has BOARD STATE as an entity — where the era's key players stand, as of a chapter, in the form the reader has seen — so every row here is hand-typed. verified:false means Shawn has not confirmed the chapters against the manga yet.
_why_this_file_exists: The voyage line answers where the STRAW HATS are; nothing answers where everyone else is. This file is the war table: the emperors, admirals, revolutionaries and wildcards whose positions ARE the state of the story. At the max chapter it answers 'where is everyone right now' for a caught-up reader; at chapter 500 the same page deals an honest 2008 board.
_semantics: A player joins the board when their character debut has passed AND their first position window has opened — both gates, because a name is a spoiler by itself. position_timeline uses the crew_presence window rule: the active window at chapter N is the last whose from_chapter <= N, unless its to_chapter has passed — then the player is 'off your chart' (position unknown is an honest state, not a bug). island_slug resolves against data/canon.json islands[] or is null for open sea / unplaceable positions. form_timeline is the same idea for POWER: the form the reader has seen by chapter N (gears, awakenings), so a card never shows a form the reader hasn't met. Coverage grows by authoring windows, not by writing code.
_coverage_note: Deliberately endgame-weighted: most windows open after Wano, because that is where 'where are they NOW' has the most value and the voyage line has the least coverage. Early-era windows exist only where they anchor a player's introduction to the board. This list grows when the research does.
_before_any_content_ships: Every row is verified:false. Shawn must confirm every chapter against the manga and flip verified to true. Until then the UI renders these as unverified.
